leadText: Chingling (Japanese: リーシャン Lisyan) is a Psychic-type baby Pokémon introduced in Generation IV. It evolves into Chimecho when leveled up with high friendship during the night.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Chingling is a yellow, spherical Pokémon similar to a jingle bell. It has stubby arms and legs, as well as beady, black eyes. There are black circular openings on both sides of its mouth. Atop its body is a short yellow ring-like growth with a striped rope through the middle. The rope resembles a scarf, and is red-and-white with short tassels on the ends. Inside its mouth is a red orb, which creates a ringing sound as the Pokémon moves. Additionally, the orb can be vibrated to create high-frequency, long-lasting cries to deafen its opponents. The sounds produced can be too high for people to hear, yet powerful enough to give them headaches. Chingling gently bounces from place to place with each hop, making a ringing sound. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Chingling evolves into Chimecho . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Chingling Psychic → First Evolution Chimecho Psychic

It deafens foes by emitting high-frequency cries. Platinum There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry DP070 Chingling Dawn's Pokédex Chingling, the Bell Pokémon. It makes sounds by vibrating an orb in the back of its throat, and it moves by hopping.
